# Medical Bill Bankruptcy - Can You Discharge? [2026]
> Last updated: 2026-05-01

> Can you file bankruptcy for medical bills? Yes. Medical debt is dischargeable in both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13. 2026 guide.

## About
medicalbillbankruptcy.com is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- After Medical Debt Discharge - Rebuilding Your Life: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/after-discharge.html
- Alternatives to Bankruptcy - For Medical Debt: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/alternatives-to-bankruptcy.html
- Automatic Stay and Medical Debt - Immediate Protection: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/automatic-stay-medical.html
- Chapter 13 Medical Debt Plan - Structured Repayment: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/chapter-13-medical-debt.html
- Hospital Lien Avoidance - Removing Liens in Bankruptcy: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/hospital-lien-avoidance.html
- Medical Bill Bankruptcy - Can You Discharge?: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/
- Medical Debt and Bankruptcy Exemptions - Protecting Your Assets: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/medical-debt-exemptions.html
- Pre-Filing Strategies - Before Filing Medical Bankruptcy: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/pre-filing-strategies.html
- Qualifying for Chapter 7 with Medical Debt - Means Test and Special Considerations: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/qualifying-chapter-7.html
- State Medical Debt Protections - Where Consumers Have Extra Rights: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/state-specific-protections.html
- Privacy Policy: https://medicalbillbankruptcy.com/privacy.html

Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
